title: Last Train
tonic_progression: C#m -> G#m -> D#m -> A#m / Bbm -> Fm (Phrygian) -> Cm (Dorian) -> Gm (Phrygian-dominant flavor) -> Dm -> Am -> Em -> Bm -> C#m
narrative_arc: Twelve tracks read as a single rain-soaked Shinjuku night, a salaryman leaving a glass office tower at the last whistle through the neon core to the final train and the long quiet ride home, the lonely Akira-meets-noir mirror of a city that never stops glowing while one man slows down. The ring closes on the same C# minor it opened on, but the listener arrives at the empty platform from the far side of the night, and the rain has washed the neon off the street.

recurring_motifs.motif: Rain cell - a four-note descending detuned-FM-bell figure (b6-5-4-3) doubled by tape-saturated rain foley, the album's weather signature
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 1 states it whole and dry as the office doors open onto the rain. Track 6 inverts it ascending under the neon-core lull. Track 11 stretches it to half-time pads on the empty platform. Track 12 returns it whole and dry one octave down on FM bell as the last sound before silence.

recurring_motifs.motif: Last-train pulse - a muted sidechained 16th-note square-wave arpeggio evoking rail clatter and a countdown clock
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 2 introduces it as distant station ambience. Track 3 doubles its density on the wet street. Track 7 drives it into a frantic 32nd ostinato at the dash for the train. Track 10 decays it to a single repeated note as the carriage thins out.

recurring_motifs.motif: Salaryman lament - a weary five-note vocal-formant lead phrased like a sigh, always landing on the minor third
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 4 plays it solo over a Rhodes. Track 8 harmonizes it in parallel fourths at the near-miss. Track 12 plays it solo and unaccompanied as the figure walks the last block home.

recurring_motifs.motif: Platform chime - a two-note major-second departure-jingle deliberately too bright for the key, like an announcement bleeding through
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 5 states it at true pitch in the neon arcade. Track 9 pitches it down a tritone and drowns it in reverb into menace at the closing doors. Track 12 restores it to true pitch as the final station jingle fading into rain.

tracks.title: Last Whistle, Floor 31

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: detuned FM bell pings over deep sub drone on C#, tape-saturated rain foley, distant elevator-hum noise floor, no rhythm

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + RAIN CELL whole and dry on FM bell, soft sub bass on root, gated reverb snare on 4 only

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + wide PWM pad, soft kick on 1, brushed gated snare on 2 and 4, mellow saw counter-line

sections.name: bridge
sections.instrumentation: drums drop, rain cell alone over pad, the office empties

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: pad sustains C#, rain cell once more, fade to rain

tracks.musicgen_prompt: cyberpunk noir downtempo 82 BPM C# minor, detuned FM bell melody descending four-note figure, deep sub bass, tape-saturated rain ambience, distant city hum, gated reverb snare, soft kick, wide PWM pad, lonely fluorescent late-night office mood, Akira meets Blade Runner, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: no text, no words, no lettering, no people faces visible. cyberpunk noir album cover, lone silhouette in a long coat stepping out of a glass office tower lobby into heavy neon-lit rain at night, magenta and cyan signage reflected on wet black asphalt, anamorphic cinematic, deep shadow, no warm sunset, 1024x1024
tracks.composer_notes: Sets the tonic C# minor and the rain palette. RAIN CELL stated dry and clear so its later transformations read. No four-on-the-floor; keep the kit gated and minimal. Outro on tonic, the night begins on stillness. Theme key 'neon_shinjuku_noir' chosen to avoid the danger-zone keys.

sections.name: verse

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + SALARYMAN LAMENT solo and unaccompanied over pad, brushed snare very soft, layered pads

tracks.musicgen_prompt: cyberpunk noir closing downtempo 80 BPM C# minor, dry undisturbed rain-cell motif on FM bell one octave low, weary lament played solo and unaccompanied, a single bright station chime at its true pitch as the last sound, fading rain, deep pad, soft kick, resolved but changed, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: no text, no words, no lettering, no faces visible. cyberpunk noir album cover, a lone figure walking away down a quiet rain-wet residential street toward a single lit window, faint dawn hinting at the horizon, last neon glow behind, resolved cinematic noir, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Up a perfect fifth from Bm to C#m, closing the ring back to the C# minor home tonic that opened the album.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -6 BPM, the album's slowest and final tempo.
tracks.composer_notes: CLOSER. RAIN CELL returns whole and dry one octave down; SALARYMAN LAMENT solo and unaccompanied; PLATFORM CHIME restored to true pitch as the final sound, with a long filter close to silence over the coda. Do not let the renderer add a fade-up tail or stinger; the filter close to silence is the composition. The ring is closed; the listener is not.

album_notes.execution_notes_for_pipeline: All BPMs within 80-118 and durations within 180-360s; instrumental MusicGen prompts with explicit no-vocals; SDXL cover prompts scene-specific with aggressive no-text front-loading. Per-track durations sum to 3000 seconds.
album_notes.key_strategy: Cycle-of-fifths ascending strictly inside the minor mode for the descent into the neon core (C#m -> G#m -> D#m -> Bbm), modal interchange through the core and dash (Fm Phrygian -> Cm Dorian -> Gm Phrygian-dominant), then a retrace home (Dm -> Am -> Em -> Bm -> C#m) closing on the home tonic. No major tonics; the night never resolves to comfort.
album_notes.motif_strategy: Four motifs (Rain cell, Last-train pulse, Salaryman lament, Platform chime) introduced across the first five tracks, transformed across the peak and comedown, and resolved in the closer, where the rain cell returns dry, the lament plays unaccompanied, and the chime is restored to true pitch.
album_notes.overall_form: A descending-into-the-core then climbing-home ring of twelve keys over a single rain-soaked Shinjuku night, departing from and returning to C# minor, mirroring one salaryman's round trip from the office tower to the last train and the long way home.
album_notes.tempo_strategy: BPM arcs up from 82 through a single 112 peak at the dash for the train (track 7), then recedes to an 80 close, tracing the energy of the night. The lone dip at the neon lull (96, track 6) precedes the peak; never crosses below 80 or above 118.
album_notes.title_safety_notes: All track titles are generic noir-scene descriptors; theme key 'neon_shinjuku_noir' avoids the danger-zone keys 'tokyo_cyberpunk' and 'blade_runner'; no track title doubles as a soundtrack-known film object or line of dialogue.
album_notes.tracklist_continuity: Each track ends on the dominant of the next track's tonic (or a common-tone modal pivot), so transitions read as inevitable rather than abrupt; sections are crossfade-friendly so the album plays as one continuous journey.
album_notes.visual_continuity_notes: Consistent magenta/cyan neon-and-rain palette, anamorphic cinematic framing, lone salaryman protagonist throughout, deep wet-asphalt reflections, no warm sunsets; all cover prompts front-load text and face suppression.
